risk_if_not_implemented: Without Memory Protection, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Least Functionality
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to configure systems to provide only essential capabilities by specifically prohibiting or restricting the use of ports, protocols, and/or services.
compensating_control_1.justification: Least Functionality (CFG-03) provides configuration hardening that compensates for the absence of Memory Protection (SEA-10) by eliminating unnecessary services and enforcing secure settings to reduce inherent exposure. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Endpoint Protection Measures
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to protect the confidentiality, integrity, availability and safety of endpoint devices.
compensating_control_2.justification: Endpoint Protection Measures (END-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Memory Protection (SEA-10) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
